item.title: 69th upvote
item.content:encoded: nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the point of the "one (1)". The majority of articles don't have this implemented, so it gives this article a sort of inconsistency that leaves a bit of a bad taste in my mouth. Other than that though, it's great! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't the containment procedures specify that tests subjects must have the same blood type ?

(+1, anyway)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any hidden meaning in the "blod" typo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If short and sweet was the goal here, I would rather see it shortened further to exclude the recovery bit, which as of now feels extraneous and kind of shoe-horned as though you were writing with the sense that even in a short piece, a perfunctory nod to backstory is somehow still required.

As it stands, I found the initial "lava lamp except with blood" part uninteresting, and the twist that surprise, it's also a thing what kills you to not really be worth the cost of admission. Additionally, I echo the comment above that I'm not sure how one determines in what precise physical location the blood is being drawn from in subjects, unless the lava lamp is punching a hole in them or something.

In some ways, hitting that old-school shortened article style while still maintaining interest in competition with 2000+ other entries is the biggest challenge on the site. Unfortunately, in my estimation the core concept just doesn't stand out enough to carry the article by its very existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, short, weird and tragic at the end too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it, simple and bizarre in a good combination.

I have one nitpick, and that is this:

its unusual mass (which is approximately 21% heavier than normal)

Mass and weight, while similar in practice (given consistent gravitational acceleration), are technically different measures- one measures mass (an innate property), and the other force.

Therefore, it would be suitable to stick with one or the other- either note the abnormal weight, and elaborate that it is 21% heavier than others of the same make, or the abnormal mass, and that it is more massive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, sweet, and subverted my expectations re: lava-lamp-based SCPs. (Also subverted my expectations re: blood-removing SCPs.)

+1

I also like how it feels complete while still leaving one with a bunch of unanswered questions. (e.g. "What is in there that's making it so heavy?" and "Did this previously belong to a young girl?")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this too short and simple to tell any kind of story or make me feel any of the emotions that I normally look for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and clever. I thought it was going to be some cliché evil lava lamp dat kill horlbly but instead was simply, odd, short and sweet. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some grammar issues, but pretty neat. I'd say add experiment logs, but a lot of people like this for its brevity, so that might be a bad idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took a couple read-throughs to warm up to this, but I like it.

One question though. Does it also transplant the blood platelets, or just the cells? If it transplanted type O blood with its platelets into an AB person, those platelets would clot the AB cells.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how this appeared to be a bad setup, but pulled the rug out from under that.

"It takes people's blood and then… THEY DIE HORRIBLE DEATHS BY EXSANGUINATION they get kind of warm. I mean, it's not pleasant, but it could be worse. And then when you switch it off ALL THE BLOOD GOES AWAY AND THEY DIIIEEE you get your blood back. And maybe someone else's. So, like, be careful and stuff."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this one does "unforseen consequences" very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did the Foundation find out that the blood was being drawn from the aorta? I might consider adding a line saying that radioisotope testing showed that the blood was drawn from the aorta, or something along those lines. Regardless, this is quite good, so I'm upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, simple, to the point, and most importantly WEIRD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That depends very heavily on your definition of "back".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, you are back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Terrific! I wanted to end that way, and then it did. Guess I'm easy to please!

item.title: Re: Upvoted!
item.content:encoded:
Basically this.

On a side note, you receive extra points for proving that short and sweet is not always bad.

item.title: Upvoted!
item.content:encoded:
… carbon monoxide poisoning that only killed one of them. Ooookaaay…

In any case, I liked it! Short and interesting. Well done. :)
